title: Permission denied when --mount'ing a directory not owned by container root (with --uidmap)
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)** /kind bug This might not really be a bug in Podman, but a kernel limitation. **Description** I'm trying to `podman run` a rootful container with a user namespace (`--uidmap`) and getting a "Permission denied: OCI permission denied" error when trying to `--mount` a directory that isn't either owned by the container root or world readable/writeable, even though the container root has full access to said directory via a mutual group. It looks like group permissions are being ignored. It seems like Podman (tbh, I rather assume it's actually the kernel, not Podman) isn't taking group permissions into account when `stat`'ing the bind mount's source directory. If I either change ownership of `/srv/storage/test` to the container root (user `testuser_croot`, uid 100000), or change permissions of `/srv/storage/test` to 777 (i.e. making it world readable/writeable), everything works fine. **Steps to reproduce the issue:** Run all commands as `root`. 1. Create users `testuser` (uid 2001) and `testother` (uid 2002) as well as the directory `/srv/storage/test` to `--mount` (owned by user `testother` and with `770` permissions): ```sh useradd -u 2001 -g 2001 -d / testuser useradd -u 2002 -g 2002 -d / testother mkdir /srv/storage{,/test} chown testother:testother /srv/storage{,/test} chmod 770 /srv/storage{,/test} ``` 2. User `testuser` is going to use the subuid/subgid range 100000-165535. Create user `testuser_croot` (uid 100000) representing the container root on the host, use group `testuser` (gid 2001) as primary group, but also add it to group `testother` (gid 2002): ```sh useradd -u 100000 -g 2001 -d / testuser_croot useradd -aG testother testuser_croot ``` User `testuser_croot` (uid 100000) now has full permissions on `/srv/storage/test` due to the common group `testother` (gid 2002): ```sh sudo -u testuser_croot -- sh -c "echo foo > /srv/storage/test/bar" ``` 3. Create a simple test image: ```sh CONTAINER="$(buildah from 'docker.io/busybox')" buildah config --cmd "sleep 3600" "$CONTAINER" buildah commit "$CONTAINER" "sleeper" ``` 4. Try `--mount`ing `/srv/storage` with a `--uidmap`: ```sh podman run -dt --name "sleeper-test" --uidmap 0:100000:65536 --gidmap 0:100000:65536 --mount "type=bind,source=/srv/storage/test,destination=/mnt" "sleeper" ``` **Describe the results you received:** ``` Error: error stat'ing file `/srv/storage`: Permission denied: OCI permission denied ``` **Describe the results you expected:** Container is up and bind mounts `/srv/storage/test` to `/mnt` in the container. **Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):** See description above.
Output of podman version: ``` Version: 3.1.2 API Version: 3.1.2 Go Version: go1.16.3 Git Commit: 51b8ddbc22cf5b10dd76dd9243924aa66ad7db39 Built: Wed Apr 21 23:34:03 2021 OS/Arch: linux/amd64 ```
Output of podman info --debug: ``` host: arch: amd64 buildahVersion: 1.20.1 cgroupManager: systemd cgroupVersion: v2 conmon: package: /usr/bin/conmon is owned by conmon 1:2.0.28-1 path: /usr/bin/conmon version: 'conmon version 2.0.28, commit: 6b18f7e0f2e4cd7f7b016b88141e82210d370008' cpus: 3 distribution: distribution: arch version: unknown eventLogger: journald hostname: SGS-CPX21-Nuernberg-1 idMappings: gidmap: null uidmap: null kernel: 5.10.37-1-lts linkmode: dynamic memFree: 3686375424 memTotal: 4019585024 ociRuntime: name: crun package: /usr/bin/crun is owned by crun 0.19.1-1 path: /usr/bin/crun version: |- crun version 0.19.1 commit: 1535fedf0b83fb898d449f9680000f729ba719f5 spec: 1.0.0 +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL os: linux remoteSocket: path: /run/podman/podman.sock security: apparmorEnabled: false capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT rootless: false seccompEnabled: true selinuxEnabled: false slirp4netns: executable: "" package: "" version: "" swapFree: 8589930496 swapTotal: 8589930496 uptime: 14h 46m 3.63s (Approximately 0.58 days) registries: {} store: configFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf containerStore: number: 1 paused: 0 running: 0 stopped: 1 graphDriverName: overlay graphOptions: overlay.mountopt: nodev graphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage graphStatus: Backing Filesystem: extfs Native Overlay Diff: "false" Supports d_type: "true" Using metacopy: "true" imageStore: number: 13 runRoot: /run/containers/storage volumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes version: APIVersion: 3.1.2 Built: 1619040843 BuiltTime: Wed Apr 21 23:34:03 2021 GitCommit: 51b8ddbc22cf5b10dd76dd9243924aa66ad7db39 GoVersion: go1.16.3 OsArch: linux/amd64 Version: 3.1.2 ```
Package info (e.g. output of rpm -q podman or apt list podman): Running `pacman -Qs podman`: ``` local/podman 3.1.2-1 Tool and library for running OCI-based containers in pods ```
**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)** Yes **Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):** Hetzner Cloud server. SELinux is disabled for now.

comments.body:
so is `/srv/storage` owned by 2002:2002 and the mappings you are using for the user namespace are ` 0:100000:65536` and ` 0:100000:65536`?

That won't work, you need to make sure 2002 (either the uid, or gid, or both) are mapped inside the user namespace

Okay, this is embarrassing :disappointed: I didn't even think about adding user `testother` to the mapping, because I expected it to end up as user `nobody`. After knowing the solution and thinking about it it totally makes sense. Adding `--uidmap 65536:2002:1 --gidmap 65536:2002:1` works.

Sorry for the noise. Keep up the amazing work. Thank you! :heart: 

By the way: There was also a mistake in my reproduction steps. Just not my day :unamused:

> Okay, this is embarrassing disappointed

no worries, user namespaces are confusing sometimes.
